_publications._publicationTitle: Quantitative MRI of dorsal root ganglion alterations in neurofibromatosis type 1 patients with or without pain.

_contextSnippet: In 8 of the 20 NF1 patients (40.0%), the diagnosis of NF-associated painful neuropathy was confirmed... Differences were found in DRG PD, showing an increase for the NF1p subcohort (median 0.932 versus 0.859 a.u., p = 0.009)
details: Used to identify NF1 patients with neuropathic pain phenotype (40% of NF1 patients, n=8/20) who showed significantly higher DRG PD values (median 0.932 vs 0.859 a.u., p = 0.009)
